title: Edition / capability declaration (v1alpha2)
description: Runtime-local declaration of the active edition and the capabilities currently enabled on an instance. TARGET-STATE contract. This is the Runtime's view (derived from a verified entitlement, or from the always-on Personal base); it is NOT the source of truth for billing. It never contains plan names or prices. Deployment modality is a SEPARATE, orthogonal field (see deployment-modality.schema.json).

capabilities.description: Currently enabled capabilities. For Personal this is the always-on base set (may be empty of gated capabilities); for team/organization it is the intersection of the entitlement and the non-degraded subscription state.

source.description: Where the active capability set came from. `personal_base`: no entitlement needed. `verified_entitlement`: a fresh Hub entitlement was verified. `cached_entitlement`: operating on the last cached entitlement inside the offline grace window.

entitlement_ref.description: Reference to the entitlement backing team/organization editions. Absent for personal_base.

subscription_state.description: Current lifecycle state driving graceful degradation. Absent for a pure Personal instance (no Hub subscription can lapse); if present under personal_base it is pinned to `active` by the conditional below, never a degraded state.

allOf.$comment: personal_base is the always-on free edition: it MUST declare edition personal and MUST NOT reference an entitlement. metadata.edition lives at the top level, so the conditional keys off it there (not spec.edition, which does not exist).

verified_entitlement/cached_entitlement are backed by a signed Hub entitlement: they MUST carry entitlement_ref and MUST declare a paid edition (team/organization). This is the reciprocal of the personal_base rule.
